title: Over-engineering when simplicity is requested
description: User asked for a repeatable way to generate personas like Vibe Builder. Initially responded with complex integration plan (work router, multi-phase rollout, validation system). User corrected: just needed simple template + command. Built that instead - template file, command spec, registry entry. Total: 3 files, ~5KB.
context: After creating Vibe Builder persona successfully, user asked 'create something repeatable'. I interpreted this as needing full system integration with routing, validation, etc. User clarified: 'I don't need all of that. I just needed something to generate persona outputs... just like we made the Vibe Builder output.'
outcome: Simplified to: (1) persona_creation_template.md - copy-paste template, (2) persona-create.md - command spec, (3) registry entry. User can now invoke 'n5 persona-create' and get a new persona. Simple, repeatable, complete.
anti_pattern: Assuming 'repeatable' means 'complex system' when user just wants 'copy this successful pattern'. Generated integration_plan.md (7.7KB), work_router_draft.md (6.7KB), repeatability_plan_summary.md (4.5KB) - all unnecessary. User wanted template + command only.
corrective_action: When user asks for 'repeatable' or 'systematic': (1) Ask clarifying question about scope before building, (2) Start with simplest interpretation, (3) Look for recent successful pattern to templatize, (4) Avoid building infrastructure when template suffices.
